computation_name: Metric Tree Radius or kNN Search
definition: Use KD-tree, ball-tree, R-tree, or related metric/spatial indexes to answer exact radius or nearest-neighbor queries under metric assumptions, then convert distances to similarities for SPECTRA.

scale_profile.best_for: Low- to moderate-dimensional metric data and spatial coordinates.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large point sets when dimensionality is low and triangle-inequality pruning is effective.
scale_profile.avoid_when: High-dimensional embeddings where tree pruning collapses and ANN is more suitable.

tunable_parameters.name: tree_leaf_size
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls index memory and query speed.

quality_gates: Verify the metric assumptions of the tree match the scientific distance.
Check whether high dimensionality degrades pruning to near brute force.
failure_modes: Metric trees are weak in high-dimensional spaces.
Incorrect coordinate projection can distort geospatial distances.
Radius cutoffs can leave isolated evaluation samples without train neighbors.
example_usage: For low-dimensional coordinates, dense_vector_topk_similarity.py can prototype exact euclidean similarity; production code can use KDTree/BallTree and emit the same columns.

citations.title: Ball*-tree: Efficient spatial indexing for constrained nearest-neighbor search
citations.evidence: Discusses exact nearest-neighbor and range search using spatial indexes and triangle-inequality pruning.

citations.title: Pyramid: A general framework for distributed similarity search
citations.evidence: Positions exact top-k search as costly at scale and motivates indexed or approximate spatial similarity search.
